summary: Iteration 3 backend testing complete - 45/45 tests passed (22 regression + 10 CAD + 13 new). Verified Deal Leaderboard (default/limit/period=week/sort), Comparable Sales panel (shape, fields, Bosque mixed_use fallback, 404), CAD discover endpoint (always returns esearch_search_url + source_label; arcgis null for Harris is acceptable), backgrounded /api/cad/bulk-enrich (returns job_id queued in <1s, /cad/jobs/{id} polls to completed with progress 9/9/9/0 for Bosque), /api/cad/jobs listing, and full regression of previous endpoints (auth, search, scrape, /cad/sources, ai-insights, single cad-enrich).

minor.issue: Iterates Mongo cursor twice to count then process - acceptable for small counties; consider a single $count or accumulate during the loop.
404 returns FastAPI default {'detail':'Job not found'} - consistent with rest of API; no fix needed.
Filters in-memory after fetching 500 properties; fine at current scale but should push adjudged_value>0 & minimum_bid>0 into the Mongo query for larger datasets.
Sort by tax_owed proximity ignores property_type/sqft mismatch after fallback. Consider returning a `match_quality` field per comp.

action_items: Optional: push leaderboard eligibility filter ($gt 0 on minimum_bid AND adjudged_value) into MongoDB query rather than Python loop for scalability.
Optional: add `match_quality` score to comparables to surface fallback (county-only vs zip-matched).

Carry-over from iteration 2: deprecated FastAPI on_event hooks, derive new_this_week from scraped_at, cad_url_for keyword extraction strips street number (may yield ambiguous matches in big cities like Harris).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py line 625: `__import__('datetime').timedelta(days=7)` is a code smell - `timedelta` is already imported transitively but should be added to the top-level `from datetime import ...` import.
server.py /cad/bulk-enrich background task: catches blanket Exception per property without logging the error - failures are silent. Add `logger.exception()` so failed enrichments are traceable.
server.py /leaderboard: `scraped_at` filter compares isoformat strings - works only because ISO-8601 sorts lexicographically. Document this or normalize to datetime in storage.

server.py /comparables: scope='zip' is reported even when zip-scoped query returned 0 results and county fallback supplied all comps (line 608 only checks `len(pool) >= 1` which is the final pool, not the zip-scoped pool). Misleading metadata.
cad_scrapers.discover_arcgis_endpoint: 4 sequential HTTP probes with 8s timeout each = up to 32s worst case per /cad/discover request. Consider asyncio.gather for parallel probes.
Property model lacks index hint comments - county/parcel_id queries (used by scrape idempotency upsert) should have a compound index for performance.

success_rate.frontend: N/A - frontend testing skipped per request

test_credentials: Dynamic users via /api/auth/register (tester+<uuid>@lien.tx / test1234). No fixed creds needed.
seed_data_creation: Relies on existing 36-property seed (Hill=12, Bosque=9, McLennan=15). McLennan provides the 15 eligible leaderboard entries (adjudged_value + minimum_bid both populated). Bosque mixed_use fallback verified.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run `pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v` for full suite (45 tests, ~75s total). test_cad.py::test_bulk_enrich_returns_job_id_immediately now polls /cad/jobs/{id} (timeout 10 min, typically completes in ~45s for Bosque). If new counties are seeded, update test_leaderboard_default's expected total_eligible (currently 15 = McLennan only). The leaderboard endpoint requires BOTH adjudged_value>0 AND minimum_bid>0 - Hill/Bosque seed data has minimum_bid but no adjudged_value, so they're excluded by design.
rca of the issue: No bugs reproduced. The single pre-existing iteration-2 issue (sync bulk-enrich could time out) has been addressed by the new BackgroundTasks-based implementation; verified the endpoint now returns in <1s with status=queued.
